concerns.text: FR-013 contains corrupted URL for KnotInfo reference validation: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])'. Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) requires all external citations to be verifiable against primary sources. This blocks verification of the validation approach, making the hyperbolic volume validation requirement untestable. Fix by providing valid KnotInfo URL or removing the citation if no verifiable reference exists.

FR-013 specifies validation against KnotInfo but does not quantify expected reference coverage. If KnotInfo coverage <90%, validation is skipped per the threshold rule. Without estimating expected coverage, it's unclear whether this validation step will be actionable or will always trigger the skip condition. Add feasibility assessment or adjust threshold to match expected coverage.

Spec acknowledges census data makes p-values inapplicable, yet FR-006 still requires reporting p-values 'for convention.' This creates methodological confusion: if p-values have no inferential meaning, why document them? Clarify whether p-values will be omitted entirely or explicitly marked as 'not applicable for census data' in all outputs to avoid misinterpretation.
